text: Generates HTCondor .sub submit file R arguments. Supports single-job multiple-job submission modes. Key design decisions: function assembles named list sections — container, executable, transfer, logging, resources, GPU, queue — writes single loop. mirrors section-list pattern established generate_dockerfile() makes adding reordering sections one-line change. mode = "single" (default) generates standard submit file queue N. mode = "multiple" activates HTCondor queue file subdatasets.csv pattern, submits one job per row manifest file produced toolero::write_by_group(manifest = TRUE). mode = "multiple", queue_from accepts manifest path directly. function extracts file_path column, applies basename() strip full paths bare filenames, writes subdatasets.csv alongside submit file. HTCondor reads file substitute $(file) throughout submit directives. $(ClusterID)-$(ProcID) prefix applied logging filenames unconditionally — even single mode — prevent log files silently overwritten resubmission. Resource presets ("small", "medium", "large", "custom") loaded inst/extdata/htc-resources.yaml runtime. local ./htc-resources.yaml project directory takes precedence, allowing per-project customization without modifying package. replaced hardcoded resource_map list Session 2. GPU support controlled gpu = FALSE (default) optional gpu_options list covering request_gpus, want_gpu_lab, min_capability, min_memory_mb. verbose comments default FALSE. verbose = TRUE, cli_inform() messages describe section written. comments = TRUE, explanatory comment blocks inserted section explaining common pitfalls. htc_gen_submit() signature:

text: drafting system-facing functions, investigated HTCondor remote submission actually works. Two approaches considered: HTCondor-native remote submission (token-based, condor_submit -name ap -pool cm) — documented HTCondor manual CHTC supports researchers. Requires ID tokens HTCondor installed locally. SSH-first workflow — CHTC’s documented approach. researcher SSHs submit node runs condor_submit . Non-interactive SSH confirmed working: Returns job queue cleanly without hanging requiring interactive input. confirmed four functions built around system2("ssh", ...) system2("scp", ...). Key constraint confirmed CHTC documentation: condor_submit must run directory submit file input files located. remote command therefore cd remote_path && condor_submit submit_file, just condor_submit remote_path/submit_file.
code: ssh lares@ap2001.chtc.wisc.edu "condor_q"

title: NA
text: CHTC distinguishes two storage locations: /home — submit files, scripts, small input files (< 1 GB). condor_submit must run /home. /staging — large files, datasets, containers (> 1 GB). Referenced submit files via osdf:/// file:/// protocols. v0.1.0, files assumed small enough /home. Staging file support (osdf:///, file:///) deferred v0.2.0.

text: Connects CHTC submit node via SSH runs condor_submit. Returns cluster ID invisibly can passed directly htc_status(). Key design decisions: remote command single-quoted prevent local shell expansion ~/: Without single quotes, ~/ expands /Users/lares/ local machine remote server receives macOS path resolve. bug caught live testing. htc_submit() captures condor_submit output parses cluster ID line "N job(s) submitted cluster XXXXXXX." Submitting job(s)... 3 job(s) submitted to cluster 6302877. ✔ Job submitted from "~/hello-world.sub" on "ap2001.chtc.wisc.edu".

text: Wraps condor_q SSH. Optionally filters cluster ID. Returns condor_q output invisibly character vector. Key design decisions: watch = TRUE enables polling loop calls htc_status() repeatedly interval seconds (default 60) cluster ID longer appears condor_q output, signaling jobs left queue. condor_watch_q considered rejected — requires interactive terminal used non-interactive SSH. watch = TRUE requires cluster_id — watching without cluster ID reliably detect specific jobs done. Completion detection: !(grepl(cluster_id, output, fixed = TRUE)). htc_status() signature: Confirmed working — full watch cycle:
code: htc_status(     cluster_id = NULL,     config     = NULL,     watch      = FALSE,     interval   = 60L,     dry_run    = FALSE,     verbose    = FALSE ) Watching cluster "6302877" — polling every 60s. Press Ctrl+C to stop. [2026-05-08 16:53:14] -- 3 idle [2026-05-08 16:54:15] -- 1 running, 2 idle [2026-05-08 16:55:16] -- 1 running, 2 done [2026-05-08 16:56:17] -- 0 jobs ✔ All jobs in cluster "6302877" have left the queue.

text: Tests written htc_config(), htc_upload(), htc_submit(), htc_status(), htc_download() following three-layer strategy documented -testing.md. Layer 1 covers argument validation paths — missing config, missing fields config, missing files, invalid file extensions, nonexistent paths, invalid cluster_id format, watch = TRUE without cluster_id. Layer 2 uses dry_run = TRUE throughout. htc_config() tests mock system2() directly via local_mocked_bindings() since SSH reachability check fires even dry_run applicable. functions short-circuit system2() dry_run = TRUE. withr::local_dir() used every htc_config() test contain htc.cfg writes. Layer 3 guards: - htc_config(): skip_if_not(file.exists("htc.cfg")) — natural guard config function - others: skip_if_not(nchar(Sys.getenv("CHTC_USERNAME")) > 0) Bugs caught testing: htc_config() validate empty string arguments — htc_config(username = "") passed silently. Fixed adding nchar(trimws(username)) == 0 nchar(trimws(server)) == 0 checks writing htc.cfg. htc_upload() used {?s} pluralization without quantity cli_abort() message: "following file{?s} exist:". cli package requires numeric quantity pluralization. Fixed changing "{length(missing_files)} file{?s} exist:". Final test counts: 153 passing, 5 skipping expected.

title: Intro to submitr
text: High-throughput computing powerful lets turn one analysis many independent jobs. Instead running loop overnight laptop, can send work UW–Madison Center High Throughput Computing (CHTC), eligible researchers can use large pools computing capacity. difficulty first CHTC submission requires several pieces line : R script can run outside RStudio; container image holding R packages analysis needs; HTCondor submit file describes job; executable shell script HTCondor runs; way transfer relevant file submit node; job submission, monitoring, result retrieval command line. experienced CHTC users, steps familiar. researchers mostly work RStudio Positron, workflow can feel like sudden jump shell scripting, SSH, SCP, HTCondor syntax. submitr designed make first jump smaller. replace CHTC, HTCondor, containers, good project organization. title: Generate an HTCondor executable shell script for an R job — htc_gen_executable
text: output_file character string. Name shell script write. Must end ".sh". Defaults "job.sh". r_script character string. Name R script HTCondor run inside container, e.g. "analysis.R". Must supplied explicitly — default. used toolero::create_qmd(use_purl = TRUE), script .R file produced purl.R rendering. results_folder character string. Name folder created inside container hold job outputs compression. Defaults "results-folder". mode character string. Execution mode. "single" (default) runs R script without positional argument, producing single fixed-name results tarball. "multiple" passes subset filename positional argument via ${1}, producing per-job tarball named ${1}-results.tar.gz. Must match mode used htc_gen_submit(). set_executable Logical. TRUE, sets executable permissions generated script via Sys.chmod() file ready copy CHTC submit node without additional steps. Defaults TRUE. Set FALSE prefer manage permissions manually, case must run chmod +x script submitting job. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages section script written. Defaults FALSE. comments Logical. TRUE, annotates section explanatory comment describing line . Useful researchers learning HTCondor executable script conventions. Defaults FALSE. output character string. Directory shell script written. Defaults "." (current working directory).

title: Generate an HTCondor submit file for a containerized R job — htc_gen_submit
text: output_file character string. Name submit file write. Must end ".sub". Defaults "job.sub". container_image character string. container image use, including registry prefix, e.g. "docker://registry.doit.wisc.edu/netid/myimage". Defaults NULL, writes placeholder comment submit file. executable character string. shell script HTCondor run inside container, e.g. "analysis.sh". Defaults NULL, writes placeholder comment submit file. input_files character vector. Files transfer job's working directory execution, e.g. c("analysis.R", "data.csv"). "multiple" mode, per-job subset file added automatically manifest; use argument files shared across jobs (e.g. analysis script). Defaults NULL. output_files character vector. Files transfer back job's working directory execution. "multiple" mode, defaults "$(file)-results.tar.gz" supplied. Defaults NULL. mode character string. Submission mode. "single" (default) submits one job. "multiple" submits one job per row manifest supplied queue_from, passing subset file positional argument executable via arguments = $(file). queue positive integer. Number identical jobs submit. used mode = "single". Defaults 1. queue_from character string. Path manifest file produced toolero::write_by_group(manifest = TRUE). Required mode = "multiple". file_path column extracted written alongside submit file subdatasets.csv, HTCondor reads generate one job per subset file. resources character string. Compute resource preset. One "small", "medium", "large", "custom" (requires custom_resources). Default preset values reflect CHTC recommendations loaded inst/extdata/htc-resources.yaml. local htc-resources.yaml working directory takes precedence package default, allowing per-project customization. Defaults "small". custom_resources named list. Required resources = "custom". Must contain cpus (integer), memory (character, e.g. "8GB"), disk (character, e.g. "4GB"). Ignored resources "custom". gpu Logical. TRUE, adds GPU resource requests submit file. Defaults FALSE. gpu_options named list NULL. Fine-grained GPU options applied gpu = TRUE. Supported keys: request_gpus (integer, default 1), want_gpu_lab (logical, default TRUE), min_capability (numeric, e.g. 8.0 A100; NULL omit), min_memory_mb (integer MB, e.g. 40000; NULL omit). gpu = TRUE gpu_options = NULL, CHTC defaults used. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages section submit file written. Defaults FALSE. comments Logical. TRUE, annotates section explanatory comment describing section use . Defaults FALSE. output character string. Directory submit file (, "multiple" mode, subdatasets.csv) written. Defaults "." (current working directory).

title: Generate an HTCondor submit file for a containerized R job — htc_gen_submit
text: Called side effects. Writes HTCondor submit file file.path(output, output_file). "multiple" mode also writes subdatasets.csv output. Returns invisible(NULL).

text: mode = "multiple", HTCondor passes subset filename executable positional argument via arguments = $(file). Split dataset toolero::write_by_group(manifest = TRUE) produce subset CSV files manifest.csv. Strip analysis.qmd analysis.R knitr::purl(). Call htc_gen_submit(mode = "multiple", queue_from = "manifest.csv") produce submit file subdatasets.csv. Copy analysis.R, subset data files, analysis.sub, analysis.sh, subdatasets.csv CHTC submit.

code: # Single-job submit file with default resource preset htc_gen_submit(output = tempdir())  # Single-job submit file with medium resources and file transfer htc_gen_submit(   output_file     = "analysis.sub",   container_image = "docker://registry.doit.wisc.edu/netid/myimage",   executable      = "analysis.sh",   input_files     = "analysis.R",   output_files    = "results.tar.gz",   resources       = "medium",   output          = tempdir() )  # Annotated submit file useful for learning HTCondor syntax htc_gen_submit(   output_file = "annotated.sub",   comments    = TRUE,   verbose     = TRUE,   output      = tempdir() ) #> Writing submit file header #> Writing container section #> Writing executable section #> Writing file transfer section #> Writing logging section #> Writing resources section (small preset: 1 CPU / 4GB RAM / 4GB disk) #> Writing queue section (1 job) #> ✔ Submit file written to /tmp/RtmpcMiCl1/annotated.sub  # Custom resource request htc_gen_submit(   resources        = "custom",   custom_resources = list(cpus = 2, memory = "8GB", disk = "4GB"),   output           = tempdir() )  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Multiple-job submit file driven by a write_by_group() manifest htc_gen_submit(   output_file     = "analysis.sub",   container_image = "docker://registry.doit.wisc.edu/netid/myimage",   executable      = "analysis.sh",   input_files     = "analysis.R",   mode            = "multiple",   queue_from      = "data/manifest.csv",   resources       = "medium",   output          = "." ) } # }

what: submitr: Scaffold and Submit Computational Jobs to HTC Schedulers — submitr-package
title: submitr: Scaffold and Submit Computational Jobs to HTC Schedulers — submitr-package
text: Provides scaffolding tools help researchers prepare submit computational jobs high-throughput computing (HTC) schedulers. Generates files required run containerized R analyses 'HTCondor', including submit files executable scripts, wraps system commands needed stage files, submit jobs, monitor status, retrieve results CHTC submit node. Provides 'htc_config()' managing connection details SSH connection reuse guidance. Works naturally alongside 'containr' container image management 'toolero' dataset splitting project scaffolding. 'HTCondor' 'CHTC' initial targets; 'HPC' scheduler support ('Slurm', 'PBS') planned future release.

title: submitr 0.0.0.9000
text: htc_config() creates reads htc.cfg project directory, managing connection details passed system-facing functions. first use prompts interactively username server, displays ControlMaster SSH setup guidance reduce Duo MFA prompts, writes htc.cfg, adds .gitignore. Subsequent calls read existing file validate server reachability. Returns named list username server. htc_gen_submit() writes ready--use HTCondor submit file (.sub) running containerized R job. Supports single-job multiple-job submission modes. Multiple mode reads manifest toolero::write_by_group(manifest = TRUE), extracts filenames, writes subdatasets.csv, emits queue file subdatasets.csv. Resource presets ("small", "medium", "large", "custom") loaded runtime inst/extdata/htc-resources.yaml. local ./htc-resources.yaml takes precedence package default. GPU support via gpu = TRUE gpu_options. comments = TRUE argument annotates section explanatory text. htc_gen_executable() writes bash script (.sh) HTCondor runs inside container. Generates four-element script: shebang, mkdir, Rscript, tar. multiple-job mode, passes ${1} positional argument R script. r_script must supplied explicitly — default. set_executable = TRUE (default) sets executable permissions via Sys.chmod(). htc_upload() copies files local machine CHTC submit node via scp. Accepts single file, vector files, directory path. Directories transferred recursively. remote_path defaults "~/". Supports dry_run = TRUE preview scp command without executing . htc_submit() connects CHTC submit node via SSH runs condor_submit remote directory files uploaded. Returns cluster ID invisibly can passed directly htc_status(). Supports dry_run = TRUE. htc_status() runs condor_q remote server report job queue status. Optionally filters cluster ID. watch = TRUE, polls repeatedly interval seconds (default 60) cluster ID disappears queue, signaling job completion. Returns condor_q output invisibly character vector. Supports dry_run = TRUE. htc_download() copies files CHTC submit node back local machine via scp. Accepts single filenames, vectors filenames, glob patterns ("*.tar.gz", "job.*"). Glob patterns single-quoted prevent local shell expansion. local_path defaults ".". Supports dry_run = TRUE.
